author: felix314159_
content: as some of you know, yesterday steel released the first [focil tests release](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/issues/3007). in addition to these tests, there also is a [focil hive simulator](https://github.com/felix314159/hive/pull/3) available with more tests (different than the steel tests, so you should ensure you pass both). so far we have been successfully testing with besu and ethrex, and it would be nice to have more client teams participate. please let me know as soon as any other client is ready, or feel free to post a status update so that we can get an overview of how much progress the different clients have made

author: marchhill
content: made a PR here to add nethermind mapper https://github.com/felix314159/hive/pull/4

author: felix314159_
content: i didn't see that 😄 pls ping me whenever u have to wait multiple days for sth. i will get it merged asap

author: soispoke
content: I think we should keep the bitlist. The main benefit is that it lets proposers avoid selecting censoring payloads at selection time, without it the proposer is blind to IL satisfaction and the builder is only punished after the fact, which is actually a liveness issue: you have more chances of selecting a payload than then gets reorged without the bitlist because you’re « flying blind »

author: soispoke
content: I agree the mechanism and the CR works without it

author: soispoke
content: But in this case it’s about liveness not CR

author: jih2nn
content: Assuming it's as a trustless payment, what is the reason for a builder to produce a censoring payload?

author: jih2nn
content: Even if the bitlist is good, the payload can be censoring one.

author: jih2nn
content: It's a "signal" and all payloads are expected to be IL-compliant by default.

author: soispoke
content: The idea is that it’s not rational for builders to lie

author: soispoke
content: But if they are not you want to skip them right away not wait until the payload is reorged

author: potuz
content: In addition to Thomas's point. There's also the benefit that it allows the proposer/P2P to choose/propagate  bids that are compatible with their forkchoice view. Without this, a builder that hasn't seen the right includers may even prevent other bids from propagating over P2P, so the right bids don't even reach the proposer

author: jih2nn
content: How the right bids fail to reach to the proposer?

author: potuz
content: They paid less therefore they're dropped

author: soispoke
content: Yeah the bid value is the only signal available without the bitlist

author: jih2nn
content: Such builder can just produce a good bitlist.

author: soispoke
content: It’s not rational for them to do that though

author: marchhill
content: as some of you know, yesterday steel

author: jih2nn
content: I don't see why a censoring builder behaves honestly only for the bitlist.

author: potuz
content: Yes but there's a difference on doing this from malicious behavior than just honest but unfortunate one

author: jih2nn
content: It's irrational to produce a censoring payload either.

author: potuz
content: The builder may not have seen the includers

author: potuz
content: This also protects such builders from having to pay for a Payload that will be reorged

author: soispoke
content: Yeah it means the builder is able to choose bids based on its current view

author: soispoke
content: of the ILs it’s seen

author: jih2nn
content: A proposer can choose the highest bid anyway. It doesn't protect the builder.

author: potuz
content: That's again not good behavior and on top of this if the proposer chooses the wrong bid and the bit list is included then attesters will reorg it's block

author: potuz
content: So he may as well not propose

author: jih2nn
content: No, it's not. The bitlist is not part of forkchoice.

author: potuz
content: It should be

author: potuz
content: You should not vote for a block that promises not to satisfy your view of IL

author: jih2nn
content: A payload with a subset bitlist can be IL-compliant. What matters is payload, not the bitlist.

author: soispoke
content: The rule should be the subset one

author: soispoke
content: As in it’s only reorged if the payload includes less txns than the ones it committed to via the bitlist

author: potuz
content: No, the bit list should be not of which txs were included, but of which includers are satisfied. Eg: if my Payload is full and I haven't included a single txs from includers I need to set a full bit list anyway

author: potuz
content: If it's not like this then the whole design is wrong

author: jih2nn
content: No, it happens when ILs overlap.

author: soispoke
content: That’s the design, bitlist of ILs not txns

author: potuz
content: Then an attester should know that a Payload is compatible with their view just knowing the bitlist

author: soispoke
content: at least here https://ethereum-magicians.org/t/epbs-focil-compatibility/24777

author: potuz
content: And I don't get how a subset is compliant

author: jih2nn
content: It's when a missing IL doesn't add any new transactions.

author: jih2nn
content: In the same vein, this is not guaranteed.

author: potuz
content: Then it's satisfied and the bit should be set

author: jih2nn
content: The builder didn't see that IL so they didn't set it. But when that IL doesn't add any new transaction, its payload will be IL-compliant.

author: potuz
content: That's fine, it's fine to drop this bid here anyway

author: soispoke
content: So it’s compliant by accident

author: soispoke
content: Idk why we would want that to work specifically

author: jih2nn
content: So you want something similar to PTC's negative vote? As in, what allows a proposer to reorg a payload honestly and safely.

author: potuz
content: This is attesters for the current block, they should reorg it on the spot if it chose the wrong bid

author: potuz
content: It protects the builder from eventually not seeing some lists

author: potuz
content: It lets the proposer to get the right bids over p2p

author: potuz
content: It doesn't make sense not to include it

author: potuz
content: And it definitely needs to be part of forkchoice

author: soispoke
content: High level it’s just an important part of the whole commit and reveal principle imo, you need to include the relevant information in what you commit to

author: soispoke
content: Otherwise it introduces unnecessary uncertainty

author: jih2nn
content: So the use case is a builder can be excused when they didn't see some IL that makes their payload not IL-compliant AND when they use trustless payment?

author: potuz
content: They aren't excused, they are simply not chosen

author: potuz
content: And the chain gets to see a valid Payload instead of none

author: jih2nn
content: They can be chosen but they won't be forced to pay.

author: potuz
content: With a vanilla implementation if they are chosen and attesters for any reason anyway make that block canonical, the builder pays

author: jih2nn
content: It looks similar to commit and reveal scheme but I don't think that's strictly true. You cannot derive the exact ILs that are committed. It's not 1:1 mapping.

author: potuz
content: But on the normal situation if both the builder and the proposer didn't see an includer even with the freeze time. Then the whole slot will be reorged

author: soispoke
content: Yeah I know it’s not 1 to 1 mapping but it’s additional/valuable info to improve liveness

author: jih2nn
content: I don't follow this one?
